event: View of the Pazzi Chapel courtyard at Santa Croce, Florence
description: A photograph of the courtyard and facade of the Pazzi Chapel at the Basilica of Santa Croce in Florence. The chapel facade features a classical portico with Corinthian columns supporting an entablature, surmounted by a dome. The courtyard is a grassy cloister enclosed by arched colonnades. To the left, the taller Gothic facade and bell tower of Santa Croce rise above the chapel. Cypress trees are visible beyond the cloister walls. The architecture exemplifies Brunelleschi's mathematical proportions and harmonious Renaissance design.

_meta.title: Cappella Dei Pazzi, Santa Croce, Florence
